strat: Borussia Dortmund, under coach Niko Kovac, typically employs a 3-4-2-1 formation, prioritizing defensive solidity and rapid transitions. Their tactical approach often involves direct balls behind the opposition's defense, leveraging the pace of their attacking players like Serhou Guirassy and Karim Adeyemi. Eintracht Frankfurt, managed by Albert Riera, generally favors a back-four system, often a 4-2-3-1, with a focus on possession-based play and building attacks through the center. They aim to control the midfield with players like Hugo Larsson and Ellyes Skhiri, though they have shown tactical flexibility, occasionally deploying a 3-4-3 formation to stretch opponents.
injuries: For Borussia Dortmund, key players Emre Can (ACL tear), Ramy Bensebaini (ankle injury), and Karim Adeyemi (muscle injury) are all sidelined, significantly impacting their midfield and attacking depth. Additionally, Niklas Süle (knee injury) and Felix Nmecha (knee injury) are major doubts due to fitness concerns, further depleting their squad. Eintracht Frankfurt will be without Rasmus Kristensen due to a red card suspension from their last match. Defenders Nnamdi Collins (ankle problem) and veteran goalkeeper Jens Grahl (hamstring strain) are also unavailable, while Michy Batshuayi is battling fitness issues and is unlikely to feature.
ref: The match will be officiated by Patrick Ittrich from Hamburg. This game marks his final appearance in Dortmund, which could potentially influence the atmosphere but is unlikely to alter his professional approach to officiating. While specific details on his card-giving tendencies for this season are not readily available, Ittrich is generally known for a balanced approach, allowing the game to flow but not shying away from issuing cards when necessary to maintain control.
tip: Borussia Dortmund is favored to win this match, primarily due to their strong home record and Eintracht Frankfurt's recent poor form, especially away from home. Dortmund has won nine of their last twelve competitive matches at Signal Iduna Park and has historically dominated this fixture at home, winning their last four home encounters by a combined 14-3 scoreline. Despite some recent inconsistent results, BVB will be highly motivated to secure a runner-up finish in the league. Frankfurt's struggles, coupled with key suspensions and injuries, make an away victory seem improbable, even with their motivation to secure a European spot.
prob: The probability of a Borussia Dortmund win is approximately 64%, with an expected odds of around 1.52. A draw holds a 20% chance, while an Eintracht Frankfurt victory is estimated at 16%. Given Dortmund's home advantage, superior league position (2nd vs 8th), and Frankfurt's current struggles, a home win is the most statistically probable outcome.
